settings.editor: Building confident, client-ready AEC project managers

settings.title: Practical PM certification programs designed for AEC professionals, by AEC professionals.

settings.editor:
For individuals and firms
with less than 20 PMs
Flexible, on-demand training program for busy AEC professionals who want to upskill on their own schedule.
Curriculum (5 PDHs)

• Project Planning & Scoping: Defining project objectives and managing scope from the start.
  • Budgeting & Financial Forecasting: Mastering fee structures, breakeven analysis, and financial fundamentals.
  • Monitoring, Controlling & Closing: Handling scope creep, managing changes, and final closeout checklists.
  • Project Communication Strategies: Practical strategies for effective internal and external communication.
  • This structured course offers short video lessons, module quizzes, knowledge checks, and an applied capstone.
Learn More

For firms
with 20-49 PMs
A fast-track, structured program for small teams to gain essential, AEC-specific PM skills that improve project outcomes.
Structure (7.5 PDHs)

• Company-only live cohorts for firms with 20+ PMs
  • Same curriculum as the self-paced Project Management Accelerator™, with guided discussions that enrich learning 
  • Spaced repetition over 3 months to improve retention and avoid bootcamp-style overload
  • Small cohort size to facilitate interactions that build peer relationships
  • Applied assignments to unlock transfer of learning to the job

For firms
with 50 or more PMs
A strategic, custom solution for firms seeking organization-wide consistency and a dynamic recruiting and retention tool.
Benefits

• Customized Due Diligence: Initial survey and dialogue to identify your firm’s specific management gaps
  • Full custom design and delivery of curriculum tailored to your organizational needs
  • AEC PM Toolkit (company-branded templates) to drive immediate consistency
  • AEC PM Behaviors Assessment to personalize the training to each PM’s needs and outline a path toward long-term growth.
  • Reinforcement and skill transfer phases built-in for lasting knowledge application
  • PDHs for your PMs
Learn More

settings.title: How We Set AEC Firms and Professionals Up for Success

settings.editor:
Our programs teach PMs the essentials of project management, from transitioning into a PM role to project scoping, budgeting, scheduling, communication, and closeout.

A brief assignment is completed after every session.

After completing the course, PMs take a 25-question certification exam to earn the official AEC PM Certification.

PMs can now demonstrate their credibility and leadership potential.

PMs receive two years of AEC PM Assist, which includes:

✅ PM Assist Micro - 40 short videos released over two years, reinforcing key concepts.

✅ PM Assist Live - Quarterly webinars with industry experts on topics like AI in project management, risk management, and more (8 PDH credits total).

❝
Like many firms, our team is technically strong, but stepping into a project management role requires a completely different skill set.

We had tried internal training, but it wasn’t enough to prepare our team for the communication, negotiation, and client-facing responsibilities that drive project success.

EMI stood out because they understand our industry and don’t take a one-size-fits-all approach. They worked with us to build a customized program that directly addressed our challenges.

Since the training, we’ve seen increased confidence in our project managers and heard firsthand how they’re applying these skills in real situations. Just as importantly, EMI made the entire process easy. They listened, understood our needs quickly, and delivered exactly what we were looking for.

It was a very positive experience, and I would highly recommend them.
❞
Jeanne Cotter
Vice President People and Process | Urban Engineers
Discover how your AEC firm can achieve long-term growth and success

slides.content: This course provided a lot of strategies to help me with defining scope and schedule, kicking off projects on the right foot, and effectively managing project completion and client expectations.
slides.name: Benjamin Weishaar
slides.title: Rodgers

slides.content: The project management accelerator - PM Skills course really helped me identify and understand the key aspects of successful project management as well as provide engaging exercises to practice these skills.
slides.name: Stuart Rodriguez
slides.title: Rodgers

slides.content: I feel like the program helped reinforce the concepts I've been learning as an active project manager. It also helped fill in any of the gaps in my knowledge. It was easy to see how I could apply the concepts to future projects and how I could have used this information to help my current projects progress smoother.
slides.name: Jon Batchelor
slides.title: TranSystems

slides.content: The JHA Project Leaders Development Program has been a great help in growing my understanding of what it takes to become a Leader. Anthony and Mike are knowledgeable and engaging instructors. The class not only improved my leadership skills, but is a great motivation tool. I highly recommend this class to anyone who wants to grow and become a more effective and successful project leader.
slides.name: Ryan Lee
slides.title: JHA

slides.content: The PM fundamentals course was a great refresher for improving on my current PM skillset. I learned a lot of new information and I acquired tools I can use to help more effectively manage my projects
slides.name: Kenny Dodd
slides.title: Parametrix

slides.content: Gave me new ideas to become a more effective project manager and raised my confidence level at honing this skill set.
slides.name: Jason Meier
slides.title: MSA

slides.content: Gave me a lot of good concepts and tools (spreadsheets/checklists) to help manage projects
slides.name: Josh Bauer
slides.title: TranSystems

slides.content: I'm very excited to start applying earned value management on my projects!
slides.name: Jimmy Jessup
slides.title: Parametrix

slides.content: The PM 2 program serves as a comprehensive document of the various responsibilities of project managers that are needed to increase the likelihood of person, team, and firm success. Taking this course helped me to recognize some of my blind spots that I frequently overlook in my regular work. I leave this course having a greater perspective of how to deal with clients and project teams, rather than focusing on only the project work at hand.
slides.name: Andrew Lee
slides.title: Parametrix

slides.content: It helped me learn how to establish and keep client relationships, manage timelines and budgets, and create strong project plans and scopes.
slides.name: Shannon Gapp
slides.title: MSA

slides.content: The TranSystems Project Management Development Program - Foundations of Project Management helped me to better understanding the expectations for PMs across the company as I start out in my PM role, but also allowed me to feel more engaged with TranSystems, as a member of a recent acquisition.
slides.name: Megan Duckett
slides.title: TranSystems
